CheckTitle: Review app registrations requesting high-privilege Graph API permissions
Description: Identifies app registrations that declare high-privilege Microsoft Graph application permissions (Role type) in their requiredResourceAccess, such as RoleManagement.ReadWrite.All, Application.ReadWrite.All, or Mail.ReadWrite.
Risk: Apps requesting high-privilege application permissions can act tenant-wide without user context if those permissions are consented. Even if not yet granted, the declared intent signals over-scoping and violates least privilege.

Remediation.Recommendation: Review the requested permissions for each flagged app. Remove unnecessary high-privilege permissions and replace with narrower scopes. Use application access policies to restrict mailbox access where possible.

Frameworks.source: AI-generated mapping — not yet verified against published benchmark
AI-generated mapping — not yet verified against published benchmark

SourceNotes: Framework control IDs were AI-generated. High-privilege permission IDs are well-known Microsoft Graph role GUIDs.
